checks.finding: All architectural decisions in design.md carry stated rationale: TARGETS map 'mirroring tests/guard-ps1-ascii.tests.sh's established TARGET->TARGETS generalization pattern' (design.md:417-419); the --effort-control-* disambiguating-flag decision is justified as 'more fragile across the .sh (POSIX empty-string) and .ps1 (native $null) twins than an explicit, typed second flag' (design.md:572-578); the ADR-0012-narrows-rather-than-rewrites-ADR-0003 decision cites 'this repository's established append-only ADR convention (no existing ADR in docs/adr/ is observed to have been retroactively rewritten...)' (design.md:579-585). frontend-spec.md's Technology Stack table additionally carries an explicit Rationale column for every layer/technology row. No bare 'we will use X' decision found without accompanying justification.

design.md's ## Open Questions section (line 718-725) states 'None blocking. All investigation.md OQ-001..OQ-004 are resolved above with design decisions' and explains the two additional (--effort-control-* flag, ADR-0012-as-new-ADR) decisions are resolved, not left open, with stated reasoning (reversible, low-risk, additive). No unresolved entry lacking an owner, blocks-implementation field, or resolution path exists.

investigation.md is present. design.md's 5 Assumptions (lines 699-717) are each grounded: eligibility-filter/escalation-logic stability cites select-agent-model.sh:232-247/:155-184 directly; .codex/agents/*.toml freedom-from-model/effort-keys traces to INV-004; the A3 ancestor-commit fact traces to INV-010/requirements.md Assumptions and cites the exact git merge-base verification; _PROTECTED_GATE_SUFFIXES stability traces to INV-006; the round-2-added .github/workflows/test.yml protection assumption cites guard_invariants.py:4 directly and states it is 're-verified... not a permanent guarantee' with a per-task re-verification procedure. I independently confirmed docs/adr/0011-phase2-handle-relative-protected-copy.md exists and 0012 is free via `ls docs/adr/`, matching the design's own verification claim.

design.md has a ## Constraint Compliance section (line 674) with 18 rows. Cross-checked against every constraint surface in requirements.md: Security Boundaries B1-B4 (rows 4/5/6/7/9/10/11), Edge Cases (bash-3.2 set -u, macOS $TMPDIR, Windows jq CRLF, protected-file boundaries incl. round-2 test.yml addition, effort_degraded_reason vacuity, xhigh/escalation-bump, v1-zero-behavioral-change) all map to explicit rows (15/16/17/5/7/9/3/1-2 respectively). No requirements.md constraint found unaddressed. The round-2 ADR renumbering did not touch this table's content, so this check's validity is unaffected by the round-2 diff.

No performance requirements found in requirements.md. This is CLI/script/contract-schema tooling (requirements.md Overview; acceptance-tests.md Notes: 'this is contract/script/test-infrastructure work... no GUI entry point'); no response-time, throughput, concurrency, or latency numeric threshold or SLA appears anywhere in requirements.md's Goals, Acceptance Criteria, or Non-goals.

design.md's ## Deployment / CI Plan (lines 646-672) states deployment target ('No runtime deployment', local/CI matrix per infra-spec.md Environments table), a feature-flag-equivalent rollout mechanism (--effort-policy welded default through Phase 1, flipped only by T-007 as a separate, gated release), concrete CI pipeline changes (three new suite pairs joining tests/run-all.sh/.ps1 directly; .github/workflows/test.yml registration staged via human-copy per the round-2 protected-file correction), and an explicit Rollback subsection. No database exists in this feature (Data Plan: JSON contract + run-record schema only), so the migrate-before/after-deploy ordering requirement does not apply.

design.md's Data Plan Migration Strategy (line 283-287) states: 'none for the registry (v1/v2 coexist, no in-place migration). Run-record v2 is additive-only; no v1 record is rewritten or migrated.' No relational-database column/table change exists anywhere in this feature (Data Entities are a new JSON contract file and an additive JSON run-record schema bump, not DB tables); the explicit no-migration statement satisfies the Data Plan requirement equivalent to a 'No data changes' declaration.

The sole external system referenced (codex CLI) is identified across three coordinated locations: design.md External Integrations ('None new... run-panelist-gpt.sh's existing codex CLI invocation... is extended with one additional flag', line 641-644) states purpose and scope; design.md API/Contract Plan (line 481-493) specifies the exact argv contract (`--model`/`--effort` flags); infra-spec.md's Runtime Dependencies table (line 98) states failure behavior ('graceful degrade already established at run-panelist-gpt.sh:62-69 (absent CLI -> exit 1, non-zero, not a tool error)'). Combined, name/purpose, contract shape, and failure behavior are all identified; no integration is mentioned only in passing without this detail.

Every component in design.md's Components table (lines 81-103) traces to a specific REQ via the Cross-Layer Dependencies table (lines 185-197), which maps all nine of REQ-001..REQ-009 to design sections, ACs, and TESTs with no gaps. No component, data model, or endpoint appears in design.md without a corresponding requirement. Conversely, every Goal (REQ-001..009) and every User Story role (registry maintainer, select-agent-model caller, Claude/Codex agent maintainer, WFI stakeholder, Codex-host operator, Claude Code user, release engineer) in requirements.md has a corresponding design element (API/Contract Plan entries, Test Strategy items, Constraint Compliance rows).

High-risk surfaces present: protected-file write-boundary/authorization (R-10 guard interaction), CLI-argument-injection resistance, and run-record telemetry truthfulness. Each has a named concrete validation path in design.md's Constraint Compliance/Test Strategy and cross-referenced acceptance-tests.md TEST IDs: TEST-019/TEST-020 (protected-file write/read boundary), TEST-027 (round-2, three-part staged/live/applied check for .github/workflows/test.yml), TEST-052 (CLI-argument-injection rejection), TEST-023/TEST-038/TEST-051 (run-record truthfulness, host-independent degradation). No high-risk surface lacks a named verification artifact.
